data.description:
## Context

Three production Jira-sync bugs escaped our tests this cycle, all the same CLASS — a gap at a *seam* that no test or probe spanned:

- **Inbound comments (bug 0ee6):** the inbound differ read snapshot key `comments`, but the fetcher writes the nested Jira-REST key `comment` — producer and consumer were unit-tested in isolation, so their shared shape was never jointly exercised.
- **Issue-links both directions (bug 3f04):** outbound link dispatch existed only in a *typed-leaf* applier that production does not use (production routes through the *batch* applier, which dropped links); inbound links were invisible because the snapshot never carried `issuelinks`.
- **A live e2e probe encoded the inbound-comment bug as EXPECTED** (`Phase3.verify-inbound-comments-gap … NOT-SYNCED`), so the defect could never be detected.

Root causes: (1) no producer↔consumer contract — differs are unit-tested against hand-built snapshot dicts in shapes the real fetcher never emits; (2) two outbound applier paths drift (tests cover the dead typed leaf; production uses the batch path; capabilities were re-added one bug at a time — parent 8b25, comments 87e4, links 3f04); (3) sub-op success is silent (the batch outcome reports mutation-level `error=None`, never `links_applied`/`comments_applied`); (4) live probes are manual, not in CI, do not round-trip relationships, and can encode bugs as expected.

Deep research (Pact consumer-driven contracts; vcrpy semantic matchers; Schemathesis + Dredd/Gavel structure-not-value validation; Google SWE-book "verified fakes" and its change-detector warning; and how real Jira clients test — `jira-python` uses a live Dockerized/Cloud sandbox, while `jira-cli` serves on-disk fixtures *captured from real Jira* THROUGH its production serialization via an in-process mock) converges on a layered, small-team-appropriate strategy: **one producer↔consumer snapshot contract test, fed by hermetic-but-honest fixtures derived from real Jira payloads, exercised through the PRODUCTION code path** — explicitly avoiding change-detector bloat. Full live-sandbox e2e and cross-team Pact are overkill for a single team.

## Success Criteria

- Producer (fetcher + enrichment) and consumers (differs/appliers) can no longer drift on snapshot shape without a test failing.
- Outbound sync is exercised through the production code path, not a parallel dead path.
- A silent sub-op no-op (e.g. links computed but never applied) fails a test/canary instead of passing green.
- Bidirectional round-trips (comments, links, parent) for the real Jira shapes are asserted, and no probe encodes a bug as expected.
- New tests assert behavior/contract (semantic round-trip, structure/type) — the suite stays lean; zero change-detector tests added.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] A shared, single-source-of-truth snapshot-entry schema exists; both producer output and consumer reads validate against it.
- [ ] One round-trip contract test exercises fetcher → snapshot → differs → applier through the production path, using fixtures derived from real Jira payloads.
- [ ] The dual outbound applier paths are collapsed to one (the production path); the retired path is deleted.
- [ ] Sub-op telemetry (links/comments/labels applied) is surfaced, and a `computed > 0 && applied == 0` condition fails loudly.
- [ ] Bidirectional comment/link/parent round-trip is asserted hermetically; every "expected-bug" probe guard is converted to fail-when-the-gap-closes.
- [ ] A documented anti-change-detector guardrail governs new tests (no mock-call-count, no whole-blob golden equality; assert semantic round-trip + structure/type).
- [ ] All child stories closed; full reconciler suite green; no net increase in brittle tests.
data.title: Close the Jira-sync producer<->consumer & tested-vs-shipped test-coverage gap class
